name: White Chocolate Glazed Anisette Biscotti
recipeIngredient: 2 tbsp Anise seed; crushed with a mortar and pestle
recipeInstructions:
1. Place nuts in a shallow pan. Bake at 350°F (175°C) for 8 to 10 minutes, or until golden brown. While still warm, rub the nuts between a double layer of paper towels to remove their papery skin. Cool.
2. Chop into halves or thirds.
3. In a small bowl, beat eggs, vanilla, and almond extract with a whisk.
4. In a large mixing bowl, combine flour, sugar, crushed anise seed, baking soda, and salt. Add egg mixture and mix until blended, about 1 minute.
5. Mix in nuts. Divide dough into two portions.
6. On a greased and floured baking sheet, form two logs that are about 1/2 inch thick, 1 1/2 inches wide, and 12 inches long. Space the logs at least 2 inches apart.
7. Bake in the middle of a preheated oven set to 325°F (165°C) for 25 minutes, or until set and baked through. Transfer from the baking sheet to a wire rack and let cool for 5 to 10 minutes.
8. Place on a cutting board. With a serrated knife, cut 1/2-inch thick slices diagonally at a 45-degree angle.
9. Lay slices flat on the baking sheet and return to the oven for 10 minutes, turning over once. Cool on the wire rack.
10. To make glaze, heat white chocolate chips over hot water in a microwave oven on Medium power, checking every 30 seconds until the chocolate melts. Stir to blend. With a spatula, spread the chocolate over the entire top surface of the cookies. Let cool at room temperature until set. Yield about 3 1/2 dozen.
